authoritySource: Dr. Ahmed Hesham — OET Speaking practice rubric for AI Conversation

criteria.title: Intelligibility
criteria.description: How easily the listener can understand the candidate: pronunciation of phonemes, word stress, sentence stress, clarity of connected speech.

criteria.title: Fluency
criteria.description: Pace, smoothness, linking, minimal filler/hesitation, ability to sustain speech without excessive pauses or false starts.

criteria.title: Appropriateness
criteria.description: Role fidelity, professional register, empathy, patient-centred communication, signposting, safety-netting, and structure (including ISBAR for handover).

criteria.title: Grammar & Expression
criteria.description: Accuracy and range of spoken grammar, appropriate use of conditionals, modals, tenses for clinical contexts, precision of clinical vocabulary without jargon overload.

scoringProjection.description: Project the mean of the 4 criteria (each 0–6) onto OET Speaking 0–500. Anchors:

anchors.note: PASS — universal Speaking Grade B (70% of max)

scoringProjection.notes: These scores are advisory and NEVER replace expert-graded Speaking attempts. The pass anchor at 4.2/6 ≡ 350/500 is mission-critical and mirrors the Pronunciation anchor at 70/100 ≡ 350/500.
